explanation: The requirement states that upon start-up (i.e., at the initial state), the system should move from prop_0 to prop_1. This means that if prop_0 holds at the initial moment, then at the next step, prop_1 should hold. In LTL, this can be written as: if prop_0 is true now, then X(prop_1) (next, prop_1 will be true).

The requirement says that prop_0 should occur when prop_1 becomes prop_2. This often means that whenever prop_1 becomes true (and prop_2 is true), then prop_0 must subsequently happen. Without specific relationships between prop_1 and prop_2, the most straightforward translation is: whenever (prop_1 & prop_2) holds, then eventually prop_0 must happen.

This means that at the very beginning (start-up), prop_0 must hold, and then prop_1 must also hold. A standard way to capture this in LTL is to require that prop_0 holds at the initial state, and that afterwards prop_1 is true (at the next step).

The requirement can be interpreted as: when prop_1 becomes true, prop_2 must happen, and after prop_2, prop_0 must happen. In LTL, this means globally, if prop_1 holds, then eventually prop_2 occurs, and after that, eventually prop_0 happens. This can be written as: G(prop_1 -> (F(prop_2 & F(prop_0)))).
output_LTL: G((StartUpMode) -> (F((guiConnected & (logParams | logVentilatorSettings | logAlarmSettings | logAlarmParams | logPowerSupply | logCalibrationParams | logVentilationParams | logPreUseCheck | logPatientChange)) & F((loadLog)))))

explanation: This requirement states that when prop_1 occurs, prop_2 should happen, and then the system performs prop_0. In LTL, this means that whenever prop_1 becomes true, eventually prop_2 becomes true, and then eventually prop_0 becomes true (possibly after prop_2). We represent this as: globally, if prop_1 holds, then eventually (prop_2 & eventually prop_0).
output_LTL: G ((StartUpMode) -> F ((guiConnected & (logParams | logAlarmParams | logVentilatorSettings | logVentilationParams | logPowerSupply | logCalibrationParams | logPreUseCheck | logAlarmSettings | logPatientChange)) & F (loadLog)))

explanation: The requirement's meaning is ambiguous, but interpreting 'The system shall prop_0 upon prop_1 to be prop_2' as 'Whenever prop_1 happens, then until prop_2 happens, prop_0 must hold.' This translates to: whenever prop_1 becomes true, prop_0 must hold at least until prop_2 becomes true.
This requirement seems to state that whenever prop_1 becomes true, the system should exhibit prop_0 until prop_2 occurs. That is, after prop_1, prop_0 must hold at least until prop_2 holds.
output_LTL: G ((StartUpMode & !startUpDone) -> ((loadLog | loadLastParams) U (guiConnected & (logParams | logVentilatorSettings | logAlarmParams | logCalibrationParams | logPreUseCheck | logPatientChange | logAlarmSettings | logVentilationParams | logPowerSupply))))

explanation: The phrase suggests that 'the system shall prop_0 upon prop_1 to be prop_2' means that as soon as prop_1 becomes prop_2 (i.e., when prop_1 & prop_2 holds), prop_0 should occur. In temporal logic, this is usually expressed as 'whenever prop_1 & prop_2 becomes true, eventually prop_0 must occur.'
output_LTL: G(((StartUpMode) & (guiConnected & (logParams | logAlarmParams | logVentilatorSettings | logVentilationParams | logAlarmSettings | logCalibrationParams | logPreUseCheck | logPatientChange | logPowerSupply))) -> F (loadLog))

explanation: This statement is incomplete or unclear in meaning ('shall prop_0 to be prop_1'), so I interpret it as: whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold at the same time, i.e., globally, prop_0 implies prop_1.

This requirement says that if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must hold up until (and including) when prop_2 occurs. This is the classic 'p holds from q until r' pattern, which is represented using the Release operator or the Until operator in LTL. Here, 'upon prop_1 to prop_2' means that from the point where prop_1 is true, prop_0 must hold at least until (and possibly at) the point where prop_2 becomes true.
output_LTL: G ((initStart | StartUpMode | selfTestPass) -> ((loadLog | loadLastParams) U ((logPreUseCheck | logVentilationParams | logAlarmSettings | logPowerSupply | logVentilatorSettings | logPatientChange | logCalibrationParams | logParams | logAlarmParams | saveLog) & guiConnected)))
